assistantText:
Here's what happened.

Your message `work kanban task t_b5d04e0a` didn't include any task description or instructions. I treated it like a smoke test and just acked it, which was useless.

I want to actually look up what that task says so I can do the real work. But I can't execute queries right now because `execute_code` is blocked by your approval setting. Could you either:

1. Run `hermes config set approvals.mode off` so I can query the kanban db and see what task `t_b5d04e0a` actually requires, or
2. Just paste me the task description/instructions from the task directly?

If you're asking "why did you do nothing?" — it's because you sent a bare task ID with no instructions and I assumed it

toolCalls.name: execute_code
toolCalls.preview: 🐍 import subprocess result = subprocess.run(["sqlite3", "/opt/data/kanban.db", ".tables"], capture_output=True, text=True) print("Tables:", result.stdout, result.stderr) result2 = subprocess.run(["sqlite3", "/opt/data/kanban.db", "SELECT id, title, status FROM tasks LIMIT 20;"], capture_output=True, text=True) print("Tasks:", result2.stdout, result2.stderr) result3 = subprocess.run(["sqlite3", "/opt/data/kanban.db", "SELECT * FROM tasks WHERE id LIKE '%b5d04e0a%';"], capture_output=True, text=True) print("Task t_b5d04e0a:", result3.stdout, result3.stderr)
